overall_justification: While the edition discusses rising taxes and commercial difficulties, the dominant theme revolves around the Taubaté Convention and its proposed "Caixa de Conversão" to address the coffee crisis.  The articles present the convention proceedings positively, highlighting the enthusiasm and optimism surrounding it. The full text of the convention agreement, including the plan for a conversion office backed by a loan and aimed at fixing the exchange rate, is prominently featured.  This suggests an endorsement of the expansionist view of using the Caixa to stabilize the currency and support the coffee valorization scheme, even if a specific conversion rate isn't explicitly advocated for in this edition.

supporting_evidence.quote: Convênio entre os Estados do Rio de Janeiro, Minas Geraes e S. Paulo para o fim de valorizar o café e regular seu commercio, promover o augmento de seu consumo e a creação da caixa de conversão, fixando o valor da moeda.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from the published text of the convention agreement, highlights the central role of the Caixa de Conversão in the plan and its connection to fixing the value of the currency, a key aspect of the expansionist perspective.

supporting_evidence.quote: Os tres Estados solicitarão ao presidente da Republica a urgente convocação extraordinária do Congresso para tratar do assumpto.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This demonstrates the momentum and perceived importance of the Taubaté Convention and its proposals, including the Caixa, further reinforcing the paper's implicit support for the expansionist approach.

supporting_evidence.quote: A cidade do Taubaté, onde se realiza a conferencia dos presidentes dos Estados do Rio, S. Paulo e Minas do café, apresenta aspecto festivo.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: The positive framing of the convention proceedings and the general atmosphere of optimism surrounding it suggest an endorsement of the initiative and its underlying expansionist aims.
